title: Is the zero-divisor graph Γ(Z_79 × Z_25) prime?
generalization_signal: At p = 79 the active four-class witness can be written directly in the live theorem language: A = A0, B = {1,211,223,227} above half the interval, and C as a size-78 complementary-support block {n <= 245 : gcd(n,6)=1} with the barrier labels removed. This shifts the feeder away from the old prime-only front and toward the fixed-A0 / high-barrier / support-avoidance slice the campaign is trying to freeze.
strongest_honest_claim: The explicit labeling for Γ(Z_79 × Z_25) survives bounded prior-art audit and direct recomputation, so the instance is a verified feeder for the fixed-A0 / high-barrier / complementary-support Γ(Z_p × Z_25) slice. It is still only an instance-level result until later family packaging and any Lean closure are completed.
candidate_theorem_slice: A fixed-A0 / high-barrier / complementary-support slice for Γ(Z_p × Z_25): if A = A0, B = {1,b1,b2,b3} with distinct prime barriers above half the interval, and C has size p - 1 with prime support avoiding {2,3,b1,b2,b3}, then the support-reduction theorem discharges all graph edges classwise.
next_action: Absorb z79-z25 into generalize as verified feeder evidence for the complementary-support slice, with p = 83 as the first pressure test for a new C-reservoir argument; only then decide whether to spend Lean effort on the isolated instance.
